# US Stock Watcher Next-Session Forecast — Thursday 7/16

**Session outlook: bullish lean — Moderate / selective risk-on · composite +20 · confidence 40%**

**Next-session forecast:** The next U.S. regular session is Thursday 7/16, opening at 9:30 AM ET (22:30 KST 7/16). This brief looks ahead to that session — the market is currently closed, and the analysis below is a forward outlook, not a recap.

*Data as of: 2026-07-16T05:57:50 · Data quality: fresh*

## Executive Summary

Heading into Thursday's open, the overall market-state score stands at +20 out of 100, placing conditions in a "Moderate / selective risk-on" regime with a confidence reading of 40%, based on four measured components. Several inputs remain unmeasured for this cycle — liquidity, credit, earnings, macro surprises, valuation, and positioning have all been reweighted out of the current calculation — so the signal should be treated as directionally useful but not exhaustive.

## Market Diagnosis

**Indices.** Going into the next session, the S&P 500 closed its prior session at 7,572.40, up 0.38% on the day. The Nasdaq-100 finished at 29,502.60, down 0.28%, while the Dow settled at 52,658.64, up 0.29%. The Russell 2000 outperformed on a percentage basis, closing at 2,976.26, up 0.39%.

**Market drivers.** Three of four tracked drivers are currently supportive heading into Thursday: volatility, index trend, and the yield curve are all constructive, while the U.S. dollar remains a headwind.

**Breadth & concentration.** The composite market-state score of +20/100 reinforces the "Moderate / selective risk-on" characterization, with confidence held at 40% on four measured components. As noted above, liquidity, credit, earnings, macro surprises, valuation, and positioning remain unmeasured or reweighted out of this reading, which caps conviction even as the directional lean stays constructive.

**Sector & rotation.** Leadership is concentrated in Financials, Health Care, and Industrials, while Energy, Consumer Staples, and Materials are lagging — a rotation pattern worth watching for continuation or reversal as Thursday's session unfolds.

**Rates, dollar & commodities.** The 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.58%. The dollar index (DXY) is at 100.51, essentially flat with a marginal 0.01% one-day gain. In commodities, WTI crude trades at 79.38, down 0.28% on the day, and gold is at 4,035.10, down 0.22%.

## Outlook

**Short term.** With the next U.S. regular session opening Thursday 7/16 at 9:30 AM ET (22:30 KST 7/16), the near-term outlook carries a constructive bias, consistent with the "Moderate / selective risk-on" market state and its 40% confidence level. This view would flip should the overall composite score cross back below zero.

**Medium term.** The medium-term stance likewise leans constructive, anchored to the same "Moderate / selective risk-on" reading and 40% confidence. As with the short-term view, a cross of the overall score below zero would invalidate this lean.

**Medium-to-long term.** Over a medium-to-long horizon, the constructive bias persists on the same basis — the "Moderate / selective risk-on" state at 40% confidence — with the identical caveat that a move of the overall score below the zero threshold would reverse the outlook.

## Top News

Coverage shaping sentiment ahead of Thursday's session includes: "The S&P 500 Has Experienced Numerous Pullbacks, but Long-Term Holders Always Have the Last Laugh" (NAI500); TSMC posting record profit as AI chip demand fuels a 77.4% surge (Nikkei Asia); stock futures edging up ahead of retail sales and jobs data, even as South Korea's Kospi tumbled 7% in a chip-sector rout (CNBC Live updates); the AI frenzy filling Asian airlines' cargo bays with semiconductors (The Straits Times); ASML raising its 2026 outlook as the AI boom drives chipmaking-equipment demand (New Electronics); and Samsung and SK Hynix shares crashing as much as 9% despite the broader AI boom (India Today).
